description: Replace the RetroUI Default (Yellow) theme with the RetroUI Purple theme across the side panel and docs site. Change all CSS variable values in :root and .dark blocks and fix hardcoded border-black references that bypass the theme system.

userStories.title: Apply purple theme to side panel styles.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel CSS variables to use the RetroUI Purple theme values so the extension renders with purple primary colors instead of yellow.
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css. Lines 35-53 are the :root block; lines 55-73 are the .dark block. Replace ALL values in both blocks with the purple theme values. The --radius must change from 0 to 0.5rem. Remove the separate --radius: 0 line in .dark (it should inherit from :root or be set to 0.5rem). Keep everything else in the file (the @import, @theme, keyframes, animations) untouched.

userStories.title: Fix hardcoded border-black in side panel Button.tsx
userStories.description: As a developer, I want buttons to use border-border instead of border-black so that the purple theme's border color (#3a3a3a) is respected instead of hardcoded pure black.
acceptanceCriteria: Button.tsx default variant uses border-border instead of border-black
Button.tsx secondary variant uses border-border instead of border-black
No occurrence of border-black remains in Button.tsx
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Button.tsx. In the buttonVariants CVA definition, the 'default' variant string contains 'border-2 border-black' — change to 'border-2 border-border'. The 'secondary' variant also contains 'border-2 border-black' — change to 'border-2 border-border'. The Tailwind class border-border resolves to border-color: var(--color-border) which maps to var(--border) via the @theme block. This makes the button borders theme-aware.

userStories.title: Apply purple theme to docs global.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs site CSS variables to use the RetroUI Purple theme values so the documentation renders with purple primary colors.
acceptanceCriteria: First :root block (theme variables, ~line 40-58): all values match the purple theme exactly
--background is #f5f5f5, --foreground is #1a1a1a, --primary is #5F4FE6, --border is #3a3a3a (and all other purple theme values)
.dark,[data-theme='dark'] block: all values match the purple dark theme exactly
--background is #0f0f12, --primary is #7b6df5, --border is #2e2e32 (and all other purple dark theme values)
.dark block does NOT contain a separate --radius line (inherits 0.5rem from :root)
userStories.notes: File: docs/src/styles/global.css. Lines 40-58 are the first :root block with theme variables. Lines 60-79 are the .dark,[data-theme='dark'] block. Replace ALL color values in both blocks with the purple theme. The exact values are: :root { --radius: 0.5rem; --background: #f5f5f5; --foreground: #1a1a1a; --card: #FFFFFF; --card-foreground: #f5f5f5; --primary: #5F4FE6; --primary-hover: #4938C2; --primary-foreground: #fff; --secondary: #3a3a3a; --secondary-foreground: #f5f5f5; --muted: #CFCCEA; --muted-foreground: #5B5686; --accent: #FED13B; --accent-foreground: #000000; --destructive: #EF4444; --destructive-foreground: #fff; --border: #3a3a3a; }. Dark: { --background: #0f0f12; --foreground: #f5f5f5; --card: #1a1a1d; --card-foreground: #eaeaea; --primary: #7b6df5; --primary-hover: #5F4FE6; --primary-foreground: #fff; --secondary: #2a2a2e; --secondary-foreground: #eaeaea; --muted: #3d395a; --muted-foreground: #a49fce; --accent: #FED13B; --accent-foreground: #000; --destructive: #EF4444; --destructive-foreground: #fff; --border: #2e2e32; }. Keep ALL other CSS (Starlight overrides, sidebar, code blocks, etc.) untouched.

userStories.title: Fix hardcoded border-black in docs Hero.astro and Pagination.astro
userStories.description: As a developer, I want docs hero buttons and pagination links to use border-border instead of border-black so the purple theme's border color is respected.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero.astro action button class list uses border-border instead of border-black
Pagination.astro prev link class uses border-border instead of border-black
Pagination.astro next link class uses border-border instead of border-black
No occurrence of border-black remains in Hero.astro or Pagination.astro
userStories.notes:
Two files to edit:

1. docs/src/overrides/Hero.astro — line ~14 in the action button class:list, the string contains 'border-2 border-black'. Change border-black to border-border.

2. docs/src/overrides/Pagination.astro — two places: the prev link (line ~30, class string contains 'border-2 border-black') and the next link (line ~42, class string contains 'border-2 border-black'). Change both to border-border.

The Tailwind class border-border resolves to border-color: var(--color-border) which maps to var(--border) via the @theme block in global.css. For the purple theme, this gives #3a3a3a (light) and #2e2e32 (dark) instead of hardcoded #000.
